overview: NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme) is a government bursary that covers tuition, accommodation, meals, transport and personal care for qualifying South African students at public universities and TVET colleges.

eligibility.sassaRecipients: Automatically qualify — no income verification required
eligibility.study: Must be registered at an accredited public institution
eligibility.academic: Must maintain satisfactory academic progress each year

applicationPeriod.note: Apply as early as possible — applications are processed on a first-come, first-served basis

fundingComponents.tuition: Full tuition fees paid directly to institution
fundingComponents.accommodation: Up to R57,000 per year (university residence or accredited private)
fundingComponents.transport: Up to R7,500 per year (for non-residential students)
fundingComponents.learningMaterials: Up to R5,000 per year (books, stationery, data)

requiredDocuments: Certified copy of South African ID
Latest payslips or proof of income (last 3 months)
howToApply: Create a myNSFAS account with your ID number
Submit your application before the closing date
Track your application status on the myNSFAS portal
importantNotes: NSFAS does not fund postgraduate studies (honours, masters, doctoral)
Students who fail more than 50% of modules may lose funding
Bursary amounts are reviewed annually — check myNSFAS for current figures
Gap year students must re-apply each year
NSFAS is a bursary, not a loan — no repayment required
